heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 93–647, § 3(e)(1)88 Stat. 2349Jan. 4, 1975
label: 1320b Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 93–647, § 3(e)(1)88 Stat. 2349Jan. 4, 1975
notes: Section, , as added , , ; amended , , ; , , , set out limitations on funds for certain social services.act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 531, title XI, § 1130Pub. L. 92–512, title III, § 301(a)86 Stat. 945Pub. L. 93–66, title II, § 22187 Stat. 159Pub. L. 93–233, § 18(j)87 Stat. 970Oct. 20, 1972July 9, 1973Dec. 31, 1973

Effective Date of RepealRepeal effective with respect to payments under sections 603 and 803 of this title for quarters commencing after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1975 Amendment note under .Sept. 30, 1975section 7(b) of Pub. L. 93–647section 303 of this title

Social Services Regulations Postponed, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 93–233, § 1287 Stat. 959Pub. L. 93–647, § 3(g)88 Stat. 2349Dec. 31, 1973Jan. 4, 1975“(a)Subject to subsection (b), no regulation and no modification of any regulation, promulgated by the Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare [now Health and Human Services] (hereinafter referred to as the ‘Secretary’) after , shall be effective for any period which begins prior to , if (and insofar as) such regulation or modification of a regulation pertains (directly or indirectly) to the provisions of law contained in section 3(a)(4)(A), 402(a)(19)(G), 403(a)(3)(A), 603(a)(1)(A), 1003(a)(3)(A), 1403(a)(3)(A), or 1603(a)(4)(A) of the Social Security Act [section 303(a)(4)(A), 602(a)(19)(G), 603(a)(3)(A), 803(a)(1)(A), 1203(a)(3)(A), 1353(a)(3)(A), or 1383(a)(4)(A) of this title].January 1, 1973October 1, 1975“(b)(1)The provisions of subsection (a) shall not be applicable to any regulation relating to ‘scope of programs’, if such regulation is identical (except as provided in the succeeding sentence) to the provisions of section 221.0 of the regulations (relating to social services) proposed by the Secretary and published in the Federal Register on . There shall be deleted from the first sentence of subsection (b) of such section 221.0 the phrase ‘meets all the applicable requirements of this part and’.May 1, 1973“(2)The provisions of subsection (a) shall not be applicable to any regulation relating to ‘limitations on total amount of Federal funds payable to States for services’, if such regulation is identical (except as provided in the succeeding sentence) to the provisions of section 221.55 of the regulations so proposed and published on . There shall be deleted from subsection (d)(1) of such section 221.55 the phrase ‘(as defined under day care services for children)’; and, in lieu of the sentence contained in subsection (d)(5) of such section 221.55, there shall be inserted the following: ‘Services provided to a child who is under foster care in a foster family home (as defined in section 408 of the Social Security Act []) or in a childcare institution (as defined in such section), or while awaiting placement in such a home or institution, but only if such services are needed by such child because he is under foster care.’.May 1, 197342 U.S.C. 608“(3)The provisions of subsection (a) shall not be applicable to any regulation relating to ‘rates and amounts of Federal financial participation for Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, and Guam’, if such regulation is identical to the provisions of section 221.56 of the regulations so proposed and published on .May 1, 1973“(4)The provisions of subsection (a) shall not be construed to preclude the Secretary from making any modification in any regulation (described in subsection (a)) if such modification is technically necessary to take account of the enactment of section 301 or 302 of the Social Security Amendments of 1972 [enacting subchapters XVI and VI of this chapter].“(c)Notwithstanding the provisions of , United States Code, any regulation described in subsection (b) may become effective upon the date of its publication in the Federal Register.”section 553(d) of title 5Similar provisions were contained in the following prior act: , , .Pub. L. 93–66, title II, § 22087 Stat. 158July 9, 1973

Modification of Social Services Regulations, , , provided in part that:Pub. L. 93–647, § 3(g)88 Stat. 2349Jan. 4, 1975“Notwithstanding the provisions of [set out as a note above], the Secretary may make any modification in any regulation described in that section if the modification is necessary to implement the provisions of this part.”section 12(a) of Public Law 93–233

Adjustment of Allotment to State for Fiscal Year EndingJune 30, 1973, , , provided for the computation of the allotment of each state for the fiscal year ending .Pub. L. 92–603, title IV, § 40386 Stat. 1487Oct. 30, 1972June 30, 1973
